segments.text: Praise the Lord! Oh, give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever!
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Who can utter the mighty deeds of the Lord, or declare all his praise?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Blessed are they who observe justice, who do righteousness at all times.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Remember me, O Lord, when you show favor to your people; help me when you save them,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: that I may look upon the prosperity of your chosen ones, that I may rejoice in the gladness of your nation, that I may glory with your inheritance.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: We have sinned, like our fathers; we have committed iniquity, we have done wickedly.
Our fathers, when they were in Egypt, did not consider your wondrous works; they did not remember the abundance of your steadfast love, but rebelled by the sea, at the Red Sea.
But they soon forgot his works; they did not wait for his counsel.

footnotes: They soon…: Heb. They made haste, they forgat

segments.text: But they had a wanton craving in the wilderness and put God to the test in the desert;
Then they despised the pleasant land, having no faith in his promise.

footnotes: the pleasant…: Heb. a land of desire

segments.text: He raised his hand against them, to make them fall in the wilderness,

footnotes: to overthrow: Heb. to make them fall

segments.text: and to overthrow their offspring among the nations and scatter them over the lands.

footnotes: To overthrow: Heb. To make them fall

segments.text: Many times he delivered them, but they were rebellious and brought low through their iniquity.
Save us, O Lord our God, and gather us from among the nations, that we may give thanks to your holy name and glory in your praise.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting! And let all the people say, 'Amen!' Praise the Lord!
segments.speaker: Psalmist
